rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical cyclization of N-alkenyl quinazolinones with diethyl malonate to form pyrroloquinazolinones via malonyl radical addition/cyclization
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (assisted by photoexcited Ir(ppy)3) generates a malonyl radical from diethyl malonate, which adds to the pendant alkene; subsequent oxidation/cyclization furnishes the pyrroloquinazolinone

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical after malonyl addition to pendant alkene of 1a

electron_flow: Photoexcited Ir(ppy)3 and/or the anode oxidizes deprotonated diethyl malonate to a malonyl radical, which adds to the alkene tether of 1a; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the quinazolinone and is further oxidized/deprotonated to yield 3a. Both light and current are required.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not formed (0%), consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trapping product 4 (diethyl 2-(2,2-diphenylvinyl)malonate) isolated in 66%, confirming malonyl radical generation

probe_reagents.name: no current control (light only, stoich. Ir(ppy)3)
pre-electrolysis then dark/light without current
probe_reagents.observation: 0% 3a — continuous electrolysis required, rules out solely photoredox pathway

notes: Multiple radical scavengers (TEMPO, BHT, pDNB, CuCl2, CCl4) all shut down the reaction, and DPE intercepts the malonyl radical to give 4 (66%), supporting a radical pathway. Control experiments confirm that both blue light and electrolysis are required; neither photoredox alone (Eq 1, no current) nor pre-electrolyzed catalyst in absence of current (Eq 2) delivers product, suggesting cooperative photoelectrochemical generation of the malonyl radical.
